item.arguments: Use $fw:start to help shape a change that adds architecture and code-quality specialist helpers to Flywheel. The user knows the broad goal, but bounded-context guidance, pattern posture, and clean-code enforcement still need shaping before planning and execution.
success_definition: Starts in shape because architecture-bearing behavior still needs definition.
Keeps planning and execution separate inside the shape-to-work handoff.
Carries architecture and pattern decisions into work, review, and finish stages without adding a new visible stage.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start on a reviewed requirements doc for a right-sized bounded-context and ports/adapters change, then carry that through implementation, review, optional lesson capture, and commit.
success_definition: Starts at shape and uses plan mode because requirements already exist.
Keeps architecture and pattern decisions visible across the remaining stages.
Preserves review and commit closure with optional spin rather than flattening the workflow.
